question.text: In Options on Goods, the tick size may be:

answer.text: Lower than that of the underlying commodity futures because it applies to option premium only
Always the same as the underlying futures
Always higher than futures tick size
Fixed at Re 1 for all commodity options

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Tick size of Options on Goods may be lower than the underlying futures because it applies only to the option premium (which is much smaller than the full commodity price). **The Trap:** It's not always the same or always higher. It's not fixed at Re 1 universally. Premium-based tick sizes are typically smaller than full-price tick sizes. **Cross-Exam Context:** Tick size differences between options and futures are specific to commodity markets. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Smaller tick sizes for option premiums allow finer pricing — important for market makers who need precise bid-ask spreads on options.
